title: Contain EMI at the source during layout
description: Minimize loop areas, control edge rates, and guard noisy nets so the board passes emissions without late shielding.
body: Use when the board carries fast clocks or switching regulators likely to radiate. Do not defer EMI to a shield-it-later phase, because loop area and routing choices set the emission floor. Actions: minimize current-loop areas, keep clocks away from board edges and connectors, and add guard traces or ground pours around noisy nets. Validation signals include low radiated emissions at clock harmonics in a pre-scan and noisy nets routed with tight return paths. Risk boundary: an emissions failure can block certification, so escalate marginal layouts to a pre-compliance scan before fabrication.
